mission.title: headlamp: Mac M1 application does not launch
mission.description: Mac M1 application does not launch. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify headlamp troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your headlamp deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp -n headlamp --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review headlamp configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant headlamp configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
kubectl get configmap -n headlamp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=headlamp
```
UPDATE: See https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/headlamp/issues/3916#issuecomment-3308142475 for the fix. The issue is the fact the app is unsigned, as called out in the [documentation](https://headlamp.dev/docs/latest/installation/desktop/).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Mac M1 application does not launch
steps.description:
- **Updated `app/package.json`**: Implemented ad-hoc signing by setting `identity: "-"`.
- **Infrastructure**: Enabled "Deep" signing to recursively sign the Electron wrapper, frameworks, and the bundled Go backend (`headlamp-server`).
- **Security Logic**: Disabled `hardenedRuntime` for macOS
```yaml
cd app && npm run package -- --mac --arm64
3. **Verify Signature**:
```

steps.title: Confirm Mac M1 application does not launch is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp -n headlamp --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n headlamp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
- **Updated `app/package.json`**: Implemented ad-hoc signing by setting `identity: "-"`.
- **Infrastructure**: Enabled "Deep" signing to recursively sign the Electron wrapper, frameworks, and the bundled Go backend (`headlamp-server`).
- **Security Logic**: Disabled `hardenedRuntime` for macOS builds to ensure compatibility with ad-hoc (non-notari
codeSnippets:
cd app && npm run package -- --mac --arm64
3. **Verify Signature**:

~> xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Headlamp.app
option -r not recognized
...

~> which xattr
/Users/sam/.pyenv/shims/xattr # <--- FML

~> /usr/bin/xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Headlamp.app
# Success

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with headlamp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
